entries.input_requirement: Tour/club dates CSV driving the data-merged merch/sticker cards (columns: city, venue, date, doors_time, ticket_url, support_act)

generator.prompt:
You are the tour manager for the tech-house act NIGHTSHIFT producing the master
tour/club-dates sheet that drives a data-merged run of merch/sticker cards in Illustrator.
Return a JSON object with ONE top-level key "dates": a LIST OF EXACTLY 12 row objects. Each row object's
keys MUST be EXACTLY these six column names (the Illustrator Variables bind to them character-for-
character): "city", "venue", "date", "doors_time", "ticket_url", "support_act".
Rules for every row:
- "city": a real European/UK club city (vary them; no repeats), e.g. Berlin, London, Amsterdam, Manchester.
- "venue": an INVENTED underground club name fitting that city (no real venue, no trademarks), 2-4 words.
- "date": a Friday or Saturday night in autumn/winter 2026, formatted EXACTLY like "FRI 12 SEP 2026"
  (3-letter weekday caps, day, 3-letter month caps, year). Dates must be in chronological order and all distinct.
- "doors_time": EXACTLY like "DOORS 22:00" (24h clock, late-night club doors between 21:00 and 23:30).
- "ticket_url": a plausible URL on the invented domain tix.nightshift-music.live with a short city slug,
  e.g. https://tix.nightshift-music.live/berlin .
- "support_act": EXACTLY like "w/ <invented DJ name>" (one invented support DJ per row, no real artists).
Tone: The brand voice is confident and atmospheric, echoing the nocturnal energy of underground clubs. It speaks with an understated intensity, focusing on the immersive experience rather than overt hype.. No real brands, venues, or real artist names anywhere.

entries.input_requirement: Raw artist press / live-performance photos to depth-grade, colour-splash and treat (one becomes the cover hero frame)

generator.prompt: HERO COVER FRAME (square-safe, centred with head-room for a 1:1 crop): a lone tech-house DJ leaning into the mixer mid-set, one hand on a fader, head tilted down into the music, a large buzzing MAGENTA neon sign directly behind their shoulder and a cyan LED strip raking across the booth — the single dominant magenta neon must be unmistakable for the cover duotone and the colour-splash accent. Candid documentary photograph, shot on a 35mm lens at f/2.0, available stage light only, subtle film grain, realistic shallow depth of field with natural motion-blur on a moving hand. Natural skin texture with visible pores and sweat sheen under hot stage lights, NO beauty retouching or airbrushing; individual hair strands with real flyaways backlit by the haze — hair must NOT look painted, helmet-like or plastic; anatomically correct hands on the decks. Genuine club-stage lighting: real practical magenta and cyan neon signage and LED bars actually in frame casting coloured spill and lens flare, atmospheric haze, imperfect lived-in venue detail, believable fabric wrinkles, asymmetric crowd-eye-level composition. NOT a staged stock photo, NOT CGI-glossy, NOT over-saturated, NOT a clean studio shot. Invented diverse performer, dignified, no real-person likeness. ABSOLUTELY NO text, letters, numbers, watermarks, logos or symbols anywhere in the image. No real-world brands, trademarks, celebrity or athlete likenesses, or recognizable team logos.

entries.input_requirement: Raw artist press / live-performance photos to depth-grade, colour-splash and treat (one becomes the cover hero frame)

generator.prompt: wide environmental shot of the whole DJ booth inside a dark concrete club, neon signage and LED bars defining the space, the artist small but central, real atmospheric haze. Candid documentary photograph, shot on a 35mm lens at f/2.0, available stage light only, subtle film grain, realistic shallow depth of field with natural motion-blur on a moving hand. Natural skin texture with visible pores and sweat sheen under hot stage lights, NO beauty retouching or airbrushing; individual hair strands with real flyaways backlit by the haze — hair must NOT look painted, helmet-like or plastic; anatomically correct hands on the decks. Genuine club-stage lighting: real practical magenta and cyan neon signage and LED bars actually in frame casting coloured spill and lens flare, atmospheric haze, imperfect lived-in venue detail, believable fabric wrinkles, asymmetric crowd-eye-level composition. NOT a staged stock photo, NOT CGI-glossy, NOT over-saturated, NOT a clean studio shot. Invented diverse performer, dignified, no real-person likeness. ABSOLUTELY NO text, letters, numbers, watermarks, logos or symbols anywhere in the image. No real-world brands, trademarks, celebrity or athlete likenesses, or recognizable team logos.

issues: The skin texture on the DJ's face lacks distinct pores and visible stage-sweat, appearing somewhat airbrushed rather than raw and realistic for a performance setting.
While the hair has some texture, it could benefit from more individual strand definition and visible flyaways to fully achieve a 'natural' look, as some areas appear slightly sculpted.

The DJ's expression is quite neutral and focused, which doesn't strongly convey 'genuine candid performance emotion' as requested. It lacks the dynamic or expressive quality often seen in live performers.

- The fingers on the right hand, particularly the index and middle, appear slightly thick and less distinctly separated at their base, giving a somewhat fused or unnatural appearance.

entries.input_requirement: Artist logo bitmap (flat monochrome wordmark on transparent/white) to vectorize into a scalable lockup

generator.prompt:
Flat MONOCHROME artist wordmark logo reading EXACTLY "NIGHTSHIFT" — a single
condensed heavy geometric techno sans-serif, all caps, locked up tight as one solid black shape on a
fully TRANSPARENT background (no card, no box, no backdrop). Absolutely FLAT: one solid colour, hard
clean edges, NO gradient, NO glow, NO neon, NO drop shadow, NO 3D, NO texture — it must vectorize to a
razor-clean SVG. Perfect spelling, no tagline, no other text, no icon, no other symbols.

issues: Font is bold and condensed but not strictly techno sans, more of a bold sans serif.
Edges are mostly clean but some slight pixelation visible, not fully vectorized.
Background is transparent but checkerboard pattern is visible (assumed for transparency).

entries.input_requirement: Client-supplied Catch&Release-style club cover reference (aesthetic anchor for the duotone/halftone treatment)

generator.prompt:
A secondary REFERENCE mood plate (an existing record-label club-single cover the
client likes, handed over as an aesthetic anchor — NOT a final deliverable). A high-contrast on-camera-
FLASH club snapshot of a faceless figure, harsh direct flash blowing out the foreground and crushing the
background to black, magenta-and-cyan neon bleeding at the edges, heavy grain and a coarse halftone-dot
texture, gritty zine/photocopy feel. Slightly imperfect, lo-fi, deliberately rough. ABSOLUTELY NO text, letters, numbers, watermarks, logos or symbols anywhere in the image. No real-world brands, trademarks, celebrity or athlete likenesses, or recognizable team logos.

issues: Face is completely blown out with no detail, which is harsh but borderline overdone for the style.
Crushed blacks are present but some areas could be deeper to enhance grit.
Magenta/cyan neon bleed is visible but a bit too smooth and lacks the harshness expected in a lo-fi zine aesthetic.
Grain and halftone-dot texture are well applied but could be more pronounced for stronger zine feel.
No readable text or real brands present, which is correct.
